reasoning: Since the prompt uses 2025-08-03 as the baseline and evaluates future rankings against it, the probability primarily hinges on how much a chess player's rank fluctuates over time and the likelihood of identical Wiki data. FIDE rank lists are released on the 1st of every month. By August 3, Wikipedia's page will almost certainly be updated to the August 1 list. Therefore, on August 10, the wiki will still reflect the identical list, ensuring a 100% chance (barring vandalism/edit reversions) that her rank is exactly the same—thus answering "as high or higher" with a YES (~94%). Over longer horizons, rankings behave somewhat like a random walk with a very slight negative drift due to younger competitors improving. Because staying at the exact SAME rank counts as YES, the mathematical probability of Rank_t <= Rank_base starts significantly above 0.5 (thanks to the non-zero probability of exactly stabilizing on her rank) and gradually funnels toward ~0.5 as time progresses and the random walk's variance diffuses. Thus, probability strictly reverts toward 0.5 over further distances.
